de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.strategy

Class ExecutedSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.strategy.ExecutedSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    SymbolicExecutionBreakpointStopCondition

    public class ExecutedSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition
extends Object
implements ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition

    This ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition stops the auto mode (ApplyStrategy) if a given number (getMaximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecutePerGoal()) of maximal executed symbolic execution tree nodes is reached in a goal.

    If a Node in KeY's proof tree is also a node in a symbolic execution tree is computed via SymbolicExecutionUtil#isSymbolicExecutionTreeNode(Node).

    The auto mode is stopped exactly in the open goal Node which will become the next symbolic execution tree node.

    Author:
    Martin Hentschel

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      private Map<Goal,Integer> executedNumberOfSetNodesPerGoal
      Maps a Goal to the number of executed symbolic execution tree nodes.
      private Map<Node,Boolean> goalAllowedResultPerSetNode
      Stores for each Node which is a symbolic execution tree node the computed result of isGoalAllowed(int, long, Proof, IGoalChooser, long, int, Goal) to make sure that it is only computed once and that the number of executed set statements is not increased multiple times for the same Node.
      static int MAXIMAL_NUMBER_OF_SET_NODES_TO_EXECUTE_PER_GOAL_FOR_ONE_STEP
      The default maximal number of steps to do exactly one step in each goal.
      static int MAXIMAL_NUMBER_OF_SET_NODES_TO_EXECUTE_PER_GOAL_IN_COMPLETE_RUN
      The default maximal number of steps to simulate a complete program execution.
      private int maximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecutePerGoal
      The maximal number of allowed symbolic execution tree nodes per goal.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      ExecutedSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition()
      Constructor to stop after one executed symbolic execution tree node.
      ExecutedSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition(int maximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecutePerGoal)
      Constructor to stop after the given number of symbolic execution tree nodes.

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods

      Returns the reason why the previous check via #isGoalAllowed(ApplyStrategy, int, long, Proof, IGoalChooser, long, int, Goal) has stopped the apply strategy.
      int getMaximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecutePerGoal()
      Returns the maximal number of executed symbolic execution tree nodes per goal per auto mode run.
      int getMaximalWork(int maxApplications, long timeout, Proof proof, IGoalChooser goalChooser)
      Returns the maximal amount of work needed to complete the task, used to display a progress bar.

      Returns a human readable message which explains why the previous #shouldStop(ApplyStrategy, Proof, IGoalChooser, long, int, SingleRuleApplicationInfo) has stopped the strategy.

      Handles the state that the node limit is exceeded.
      protected void handleNodeLimitNotExceeded(int maxApplications, long timeout, Proof proof, IGoalChooser goalChooser, long startTime, int countApplied, Goal goal, Node node, RuleApp ruleApp, Integer executedNumberOfSetNodes)
      Handles the state that the node limit is not exceeded.
      boolean isGoalAllowed(int maxApplications, long timeout, Proof proof, IGoalChooser goalChooser, long startTime, int countApplied, Goal goal)
      Checks if it is allowed to apply the next rule on the selected Goal chosen by the IGoalChooser before it is applied.
      void setMaximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecutePerGoal(int maximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecute)
      Sets the maximal number of executed symbolic execution tree nodes per goal per auto mode run.

      Checks after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or if the strategy should stop.
      boolean wasSetNodeExecuted()
      Checks if at least one symbolic execution tree node was executed.

      • maximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecutePerGoal

        private int maximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecutePerGoal
        The maximal number of allowed symbolic execution tree nodes per goal. The auto mode will stop exactly in the open goal proof node which becomes the next symbolic execution tree node.

      • executedNumberOfSetNodesPerGoal

        private final Map<Goal,Integer> executedNumberOfSetNodesPerGoal
        Maps a Goal to the number of executed symbolic execution tree nodes.

      • goalAllowedResultPerSetNode

        private final Map<Node,Boolean> goalAllowedResultPerSetNode
        Stores for each Node which is a symbolic execution tree node the computed result of isGoalAllowed(int, long, Proof, IGoalChooser, long, int, Goal) to make sure that it is only computed once and that the number of executed set statements is not increased multiple times for the same Node.

      • ExecutedSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition

        public ExecutedSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition()
        Constructor to stop after one executed symbolic execution tree node.

      • ExecutedSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition

        public ExecutedSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition(int maximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecutePerGoal)
        Constructor to stop after the given number of symbolic execution tree nodes.
        Parameters:
        maximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecutePerGoal - The maximal number of allowed symbolic execution tree nodes per goal.

    • Method Detail

      • getMaximalWork

        public int getMaximalWork(int maxApplications,
                          long timeout,
                          Proof proof,
                          IGoalChooser goalChooser)
        Returns the maximal amount of work needed to complete the task, used to display a progress bar. Pass 0 to indicate unknown size.
        Specified by:
        getMaximalWork in interface ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition
        Parameters:
        maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
        timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
        proof - The current Proof.
        goalChooser - The current IGoalChooser.
        Returns:
        The maximal amount of work or 0 if it is unknown.

      • isGoalAllowed

        public boolean isGoalAllowed(int maxApplications,
                             long timeout,
                             Proof proof,
                             IGoalChooser goalChooser,
                             long startTime,
                             int countApplied,
                             Goal goal)
        Checks if it is allowed to apply the next rule on the selected Goal chosen by the IGoalChooser before it is applied. If it is not allowed the apply strategy will stop.
        Specified by:
        isGoalAllowed in interface ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition
        Parameters:
        maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
        timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
        proof - The current Proof.
        goalChooser - The current IGoalChooser.
        startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.currentTimeMillis()
        countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
        goal - The current Goal on which the next rule will be applied.
        Returns:
        true rule application is allowed, false rule application is not allowed so stop apply strategy

      • handleNodeLimitExceeded

        protected void handleNodeLimitExceeded(int maxApplications,
                                       long timeout,
                                       Proof proof,
                                       IGoalChooser goalChooser,
                                       long startTime,
                                       int countApplied,
                                       Goal goal,
                                       Node node,
                                       RuleApp ruleApp,
                                       Integer executedNumberOfSetNodes)
        Handles the state that the node limit is exceeded.
        Parameters:
        maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
        timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
        proof - The current Proof.
        goalChooser - The current IGoalChooser.
        startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.nanoTime()
        countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
        goal - The current Goal on which the next rule will be applied.
        node - The Node of the current Goal.
        ruleApp - The current RuleApp.
        executedNumberOfSetNodes - The executed number of SET nodes.

                                          long timeout,

        Handles the state that the node limit is not exceeded.

        timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
        proof - The current Proof.
        goalChooser - The current IGoalChooser.
        startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.nanoTime()
        countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
        goal - The current Goal on which the next rule will be applied.
        node - The Node of the current Goal.
        ruleApp - The current RuleApp.
        executedNumberOfSetNodes - The executed number of SET nodes.

      • getGoalNotAllowedMessage

                                       long timeout,
                                       Proof proof,
                                       IGoalChooser goalChooser,
                                       long startTime,
                                       int countApplied,
                                       Goal goal)
        Returns the reason why the previous check via #isGoalAllowed(ApplyStrategy, int, long, Proof, IGoalChooser, long, int, Goal) has stopped the apply strategy.
        Specified by:
        getGoalNotAllowedMessage in interface ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition
        Parameters:

        timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
        proof - The current Proof.
        goalChooser - The current IGoalChooser.
        startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.currentTimeMillis()
        countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
        goal - The current Goal on which the next rule will be applied.
        Returns:

      • shouldStop

        public boolean shouldStop(int maxApplications,
                          long timeout,
                          Proof proof,
                          IGoalChooser goalChooser,
                          long startTime,
                          int countApplied,
                          ApplyStrategy.SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
        Checks after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or if the strategy should stop.
        Specified by:
        shouldStop in interface ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition
        Parameters:
        maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
        timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
        proof - The current Proof.
        goalChooser - The current IGoalChooser.
        startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.currentTimeMillis()
        countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
        singleRuleApplicationInfo - An optional ApplyStrategy.SingleRuleApplicationInfo.
        Returns:
        true stop strategy, false continue strategy and apply next rule.

      • getStopMessage

        public String getStopMessage(int maxApplications,
                             long timeout,
                             Proof proof,
                             IGoalChooser goalChooser,
                             long startTime,
                             int countApplied,
                             ApplyStrategy.SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
        Returns a human readable message which explains why the previous #shouldStop(ApplyStrategy, Proof, IGoalChooser, long, int, SingleRuleApplicationInfo) has stopped the strategy.
        Specified by:
        getStopMessage in interface ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition
        Parameters:
        maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
        timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
        proof - The current Proof.
        goalChooser - The current IGoalChooser.
        startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.currentTimeMillis()
        countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
        singleRuleApplicationInfo - An optional ApplyStrategy.SingleRuleApplicationInfo.
        Returns:
        The human readable message which explains the stop reason.

      • getMaximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecutePerGoal

        public int getMaximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecutePerGoal()
        Returns the maximal number of executed symbolic execution tree nodes per goal per auto mode run.
        Returns:
        The maximal number of executed symbolic execution tree nodes per goal per auto mode run.

      • setMaximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecutePerGoal

        Sets the maximal number of executed symbolic execution tree nodes per goal per auto mode run.
        Parameters:
        maximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecute - The maximal number of executed symbolic execution tree nodes per per goal auto mode run.

      • wasSetNodeExecuted

        public boolean wasSetNodeExecuted()
        Checks if at least one symbolic execution tree node was executed.
        Returns:
        true at least one symbolic execution tree node was executed, false no symbolic execution tree node was executed.

      • getExectuedSetNodesPerGoal

        Returns the number of executed symbolic execution tree nodes per Goal.
